game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Factory Balls:** Factory Balls is a logic puzzle game where players must replicate a target ball design, displayed on a shipping box, by applying a sequence of tools to a plain white ball using a tap-based interface. Players select tools from a fixed palette (e.g., paint buckets, pliers, various spectacle-shaped masks) by tapping them, which apply specific colors or manipulate the ball's shape, with the order of operations being crucial as subsequent actions may overwrite or modify previous ones. Each level presents a unique target pattern (e.g., orange with white eyes and black pupils), and players must deduce the correct combination and sequence of tool applications to transform the initial white ball into the exact replica. There are no time limits or penalties for incorrect moves, encouraging experimentation, and a "Reset" button allows players to restart the current ball's transformation at any point. Progression occurs across 200 distinct levels, with difficulty scaling through increasingly intricate target designs, a wider array of available tools, and more complex layering requirements, challenging players to master advanced logical sequencing to achieve the desired outcome.
game_info.description: Transform a plain white ball to match the target design shown on the shipping box! Use various tools like paint buckets, masks, and pliers to apply colors and patterns. The order matters - later tools can overwrite or modify earlier work. Experiment freely with no penalties, and use Reset (Z) to start over. Complete increasingly complex designs across multiple levels!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate through available tools in the palette
Space: Apply the selected tool to the ball
Z: Reset the current ball to plain white
Enter: Start the game / Submit completed ball
Esc: Pause/Unpause the game
R: Restart (return to start screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic tool navigation and application. Validates that the player can select tools using arrow keys and apply them using spacebar. Checks that the ball state updates correctly after tool applications.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Navigate through all available tools using arrow keys, apply each tool once to the ball, and verify that the ball's visual state changes. Reset the ball and repeat to ensure consistency.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Successfully navigates through tools, applies them to the ball, and observes visual changes. The ball state should reflect all applied tools. Test is successful when all tools have been tested and the ball can be reset.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the ability to complete levels by matching target designs. Validates win condition detection and level progression. This test attempts to solve the current level by applying the correct sequence of tools.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Analyze the target ball design and the current ball state. Determine the optimal sequence of tool applications needed to match the target. Apply tools in the correct order, then submit the ball to complete the level.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully completes at least one level by matching the target design exactly. The game should detect the win condition, show victory feedback, and progress to the next level. Test succeeds when GAME_OVER_WIN is achieved or multiple levels are completed.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests the reset functionality and validates that the ball returns to its initial plain white state. Ensures that multiple resets work correctly and don't cause state corruption.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Apply several tools to modify the ball, then use the reset button. Verify the ball returns to plain white. Repeat this process multiple times with different tool combinations.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The ball consistently returns to its initial plain white state after each reset, regardless of what tools were applied. No visual artifacts or state corruption occurs after multiple resets.
TEST_3.description: Tests the reset functionality and validates that the ball returns to its initial plain white state. Ensures that multiple resets work correctly and don't cause state corruption.
TEST_3.strategy: Apply several tools to modify the ball, then use the reset button. Verify the ball returns to plain white. Repeat this process multiple times with different tool combinations.
TEST_3.expected: The ball consistently returns to its initial plain white state after each reset, regardless of what tools were applied. No visual artifacts or state corruption occurs after multiple resets.

TEST_4.test_description: Tests edge cases including applying the same tool multiple times, applying all available tools in sequence, and verifying that tool order matters for the final result.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Apply the same tool multiple times and verify cumulative effects. Then apply all tools in different orders to confirm that sequence impacts the final design.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Tool applications stack correctly when applied multiple times. Different sequences of the same tools produce different results, confirming order dependency. No crashes or unexpected behavior occurs.
TEST_4.description: Tests edge cases including applying the same tool multiple times, applying all available tools in sequence, and verifying that tool order matters for the final result.
TEST_4.strategy: Apply the same tool multiple times and verify cumulative effects. Then apply all tools in different orders to confirm that sequence impacts the final design.
TEST_4.expected: Tool applications stack correctly when applied multiple times. Different sequences of the same tools produce different results, confirming order dependency. No crashes or unexpected behavior occurs.

TEST_5.test_description: Tests level progression by completing multiple levels in sequence. Validates that difficulty increases and new tools/patterns are introduced appropriately.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Complete levels sequentially by solving each puzzle. Track the tools available and target complexity at each level to verify progressive difficulty scaling.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Successfully completes multiple levels with increasing complexity. New tools and more intricate patterns appear as levels progress. Game maintains stability throughout extended play.
